text.title: NoSQL Patterns Procedure
text.summary: Step-by-step procedure to apply NoSQL Patterns end-to-end. Each step lists input, action, output, and an optional decision gate.

step.name: document-access-patterns
step.action: Author an access-pattern register: primary query, QPS, latency budget p95, expected dataset size at 1y.
step.decision-gate: Reject if register is empty - cannot pick store_class.

step.name: pick-store-class
step.action: Score document / key-value / wide-column / graph; pick by access shape (aggregate / cache / time-series / traversal).
step.decision-gate: Default Postgres if no pattern clearly maps onto a NoSQL primitive.

step.name: embed-or-reference
step.action: For each child relationship, decide embed (bounded 1:few) or reference (1:many / many:many).
step.decision-gate: Reject any embed whose cardinality bound is missing.

step.name: ttl-and-keys
step.action: Author ttl_seconds per prefix; enforce `{service}:{entity}:{id}` namespace.
step.decision-gate: Reject zero or missing ttl on any prefix.

step.name: indexes-and-partition
step.action: Enumerate indexes per collection / label; for Cassandra / DynamoDB pick partition key tied to the primary query.
step.decision-gate: Reject if any predicate lacks an index entry.
